titlestmt.titleproper: Riot Grrrl zine and music collection8125
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2019-11-24 00:20:50 +0000.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Riot Grrrl zine and music collection
did.abstract: A collection of more than 250 Riot Grrrl-related zines along with original cassette tapes and seven-inch singles from Bikini Kill, Bratmobile, Skinned Teen, and others bands who pioneered the movement and its sound. Topics of the zines span across queer culture, punk rock, sexual politics, the gay skinhead movement, skate culture, feminism and gender roles, covering a wide geographical area from the late 1980s through the 1990s.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Riot grrrl is an underground feminist punk movement that originated in the early 1990s in Washington state. It is a subcultural movement that combines feminist consciousness and punk style and politics.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: A collection of more than 250 Riot Grrrl-related zines along with original cassette tapes and seven-inch singles from Bikini Kill, Bratmobile, Skinned Teen, and others bands who pioneered the movement and its sound. Topics of the zines span across queer culture, punk rock, sexual politics, the gay skinhead movement, skate culture, feminism and gender roles, covering a wide geographical area from the late 1980s through the 1990s.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material is held in the Riot Grrrl Collection in NYU's Fales Library.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: These materials are from the personal collection of Matt Wobensmith, publisher of the zine Outpunk between 1992 and 1997, and curator of multiple exhibitions on zine culture.

emph.ref: Series III. Sound and Video Recordings

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Riot Grrrl zine and music collection, #8125. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Zines are arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: A Zine About Womens Self Defense

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7" vinyl record included with Ablaze issue #10. Contains the songs "Dragging Your Weight Around," "Soar Ossinaxx at Long Last," "Kettle," and "Change"

did.unittitle: Bikini Kill: A Color and Activity Book
Daughter of the Water Demon, The
Don't Be a Hard Rock When You Really Are a Gem, Baby Girl…

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Skate Like a Girl San Francisco

did.unittitle: Free to Choose: A Guide to Reproductive Freedom
Guerilla Graywater Girls Guide to Water, The
Have You Seen the Dog Lately?
High St. Este de Oakland Tierra De Lucha y Limones
"Is This Logical Evolution or a Zone of Illusion?"
Kurt Cobain Was Lactose Intolerant Conspiracy Zine
Life and Times of Butch Dykes, The

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue #1 Vol 1, Issue #2 Vol 1, Issue #3 Vol 1, Issue #2 Vol 2, Issue #1 Vol 3, Issue #2 Vol 3, Issue #1, Vol 4, Issue #1 Vol 5, Issue #1 Vol 6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On verso of Love Trinkets zine

did.unittitle: Marks in Time: The Very Early Go-Go's
Nuthin' Good Ever Happens at 4 A.M.
SF Bay Area Girl Convention Infozine, The
Spitboy Rule: Tales of a Female Punk Band, The

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: The Workers Self-Education Foundation of the Industrial Workers of the World Philadelphia

did.unittitle: Watch Out Because Next Time You Yell Hey Babe You Might Be Left Wondering What Hit You
Women's How to Guide to All Sorts of Survival Skills that Help Women Take Control of Their Lives
Xicanistas and Punkeristas Say It Loud!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers and announcements for various conferences, festivals, and tours.

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Skate Like a Girl San Francisco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes promotional materials for various organizations, record labels, and zines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Spitboy lyric sheet, a photocopied page titled "How to Hop a Freight Train," and instructions for a video chainletter project called "Big Miss Moviola."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Sound recordings are arranged alphabetically by artist name.

did.unittitle: Buildings Are the Purtiest Trees I've Seen
There's a Dyke in the Pit
This is a Tracy + The Plastics
By the Time We Get to Colorado
